text:
X-POLE A-FRAME Instruction Manual, Version 2.0 (2024)
The manual describes the A-FRAME as a lightweight portable frame designed for home and light-studio aerial-fitness use. It says the frame was not designed for professional performance or dynamic drops.
X-POLE reports independent testing with a minimum breaking load of 700 kilograms. The manual lists working-load values of 70 kilograms or 154 pounds at 10:1, 100 kilograms or 220 pounds at 7:1, and 140 kilograms or 309 pounds at 5:1. It says X-POLE uses the 5:1 value because the A-FRAME is used in a low-level training environment.
The selectable height range is 2,255 to 3,480 millimetres (89 to 137 inches). The stated floor area grows from 3.6 square metres or 38 square feet at minimum height to 8.9 square metres or 95 square feet when fully extended.
The manual warns that uneven floors can affect stability, recommends a safety mat, and calls for two people when unpacking and lifting the frame. It directs users to attach the supplied brace straps and secure all four legs with weight bags. Each clear inner bag holds about five kilograms of sand or similar material, and two filled inner bags go inside each outer weight bag.
The manual says the frame supports aerial fitness, yoga, aerial hoop or lyra, hammock, silks, static trapeze, and suspension training. It strongly recommends a choked strop for rigging aerial hoops. The frame must be stored in a cool, dry place and not left outside.
title: A-FRAME Instruction Manual, Version 2.0
